facilities.title: Master Care Centre
facilities.editorial_summary:
Master Care Centre (MCC), trading as 慈康护理中心, is a private nursing home group with three branches in Johor Bahru and over a decade of operation in the city. The founding branch — MCC@Yahya on Jalan Hassan Al-Attas, Bandar Johor Bahru — is the group's original and most established location, drawing the largest share of its Google review base. Nursing care for elderly residents is the primary offering, with post-surgery recovery and stroke rehabilitation also available for residents requiring more targeted clinical support.

Master Care Centre (慈康护理中心) positions itself as a clinically oriented group: the listed care categories across all branches are nursing care, post-surgery recovery, and stroke rehabilitation. The group's website (masternursingcare.com) was not accessible at the time of writing, so full details on bed counts, staffing ratios, and fee structures were not available online. All three branches share a common contact line. Confirm the JKM licence number at whichever branch you visit, ask whether a registered nurse is on duty overnight, and request a written quote — monthly fees vary by care dependency and room type and are not published online.

MCC@Yahya holds a 4.8-star rating across 21 Google reviews — the highest review volume across the three branches, making it the most useful reference point for families evaluating the group. Reading individual reviews in Google Maps will give you a clearer sense of recurring themes around staff responsiveness and daily care. If you are considering any Master Care Centre branch, starting with a visit to MCC@Yahya is a practical way to assess the group's standards before looking at the other locations. Confirm the JKM licence certificate, overnight nursing cover, and the fee structure for your family member's specific dependency level.

facilities.title: Master Nursing Care Centre @Daya
facilities.editorial_summary:
Master Care Centre @Daya (MCC@Daya) is one of three branches operated by Master Care Centre (慈康护理中心), a private nursing home group with over a decade of presence in Johor Bahru. The Daya branch sits at 58–62 Jalan Nipah 14, Taman Daya — a mature residential suburb in JB's Tebrau zone, roughly 10–12 km north of the city centre, served by Causeway Link bus routes in the area. Nursing care, post-surgery recovery, and stroke rehabilitation are the stated care categories across all MCC branches.

Master Care Centre (慈康护理中心) positions itself as a clinically oriented group: the listed care categories across all branches are nursing care, post-surgery recovery, and stroke rehabilitation. The group's website (masternursingcare.com) was not accessible at the time of writing, so full details on bed counts, staffing ratios, and fee structures were not available online. All three branches share a common contact line. Confirm the JKM licence number at whichever branch you visit, ask whether a registered nurse is on duty overnight, and request a written quote — monthly fees vary by care dependency and room type and are not published online.

MCC@Daya has 4 Google reviews averaging 5.0 stars. The review count is small, and MCC@Yahya's 21-review profile gives a fuller picture of the group's overall care approach — consider visiting the founding branch first. The Taman Daya location makes this branch the most accessible option for families in the Tebrau, Desa Tebrau, and Setia Indah corridors who want to avoid the traffic of central JB. When visiting, confirm the JKM licence number specific to this branch, ask about overnight nursing staffing, and request a written breakdown of fees by dependency level and room type.

facilities.title: Master Care Centre • Taman Abad
facilities.editorial_summary:
Master Care Centre — Taman Abad (MCC@Abad) is one of three branches operated by Master Care Centre (慈康护理中心), a private nursing home group that has been running in Johor Bahru for over a decade. The branch sits at 5 Jalan Dato Sulaiman, Taman Abad, 80250 Johor Bahru — a mature, centrally positioned neighbourhood in JB's western zone, with good accessibility for families in the Larkin and city-centre areas. Nursing care, post-surgery recovery, and stroke rehabilitation are the listed care offerings across all MCC branches.

Master Care Centre (慈康护理中心) positions itself as a clinically oriented group: the listed care categories across all branches are nursing care, post-surgery recovery, and stroke rehabilitation. The group's website (masternursingcare.com) was not accessible at the time of writing, so full details on bed counts, staffing ratios, and fee structures were not available online. All three branches share a common contact line. Confirm the JKM licence number at whichever branch you visit, ask whether a registered nurse is on duty overnight, and request a written quote — monthly fees vary by care dependency and room type and are not published online.

MCC@Abad has 6 Google reviews averaging 4.7 stars. The Taman Abad address gives this branch an advantage for families based in JB's central and western districts who want to visit frequently. MCC@Yahya's 21-review profile remains the richest reference for the group as a whole; visiting both branches on the same day is straightforward given how JB's road network connects these neighbourhoods. Confirm the JKM licence number, ask about nursing staffing overnight, and request a written quote — monthly fees vary by dependency level and are not published online for any MCC branch.

facilities.title: Rebina House Care Centre
facilities.editorial_summary:
Rebina House Care Centre is the founding residential arm of Rebina Sunrise Elderly Care (養身之家), a family-owned eldercare operator established in Johor Bahru in 2003. The home is located in Taman Kebun Teh, JB, registered with JKM under reference JM0763235-M at 15 and 17 Jalan Keranji. With over two decades of continuous operation, Rebina House is among the more tenured privately run nursing homes in JB's south-western residential belt.

The Rebina Sunrise group covers both residential care and home-care services through three registered entities: Rebina House Care Centre (15 and 17 Jalan Keranji, JKM registration JM0763235-M), Sunrise Care Centre Sdn Bhd (48 Jalan Keranji, SSM 567396-U), and Rebina Home Care Centre Sdn Bhd. Operating languages are predominantly Mandarin and English. The group also runs Sunrise Homecare as a separate home-care arm — a practical option for families who may later want to shift from residential to at-home support, or who need transitional care after hospital discharge. Pricing and bed capacity are not published online; ask for a written quote and a facility tour when you visit.

Rebina House holds a 4.8-star rating across 17 Google reviews — the largest and most informative review body in the Rebina Sunrise group, and a strong signal for a family-run home in JB's mid-market. Reviewers consistently note the family-run character and the Mandarin-speaking environment, reflecting a home that caters closely to the local Chinese Malaysian community. Families seeking a familiar cultural and linguistic setting will find Rebina Sunrise more comfortable than larger or more institutionalised operators. Confirm bed availability, the JKM licence certificate, and the fee structure directly when you visit.

facilities.title: Sunrise Care Centre Sdn Bhd
facilities.editorial_summary:
Sunrise Care Centre Sdn Bhd (company registration 567396-U) is one of three facilities in the Rebina Sunrise Elderly Care group, a family-owned operator established in Johor Bahru in 2003. The facility is located at 48 Jalan Keranji, Taman Kebun Teh — the same street as Rebina House Care Centre — making this a tightly clustered arrangement where the same family manages multiple registered entities within easy walking distance of one another.

The Rebina Sunrise group covers both residential care and home-care services through three registered entities: Rebina House Care Centre (15 and 17 Jalan Keranji, JKM registration JM0763235-M), Sunrise Care Centre Sdn Bhd (48 Jalan Keranji, SSM 567396-U), and Rebina Home Care Centre Sdn Bhd. Operating languages are predominantly Mandarin and English. The group also runs Sunrise Homecare as a separate home-care arm — a practical option for families who may later want to shift from residential to at-home support, or who need transitional care after hospital discharge. Pricing and bed capacity are not published online; ask for a written quote and a facility tour when you visit.

Sunrise Care Centre has 3 Google reviews averaging 5.0 stars. The rating is strong, but the review count is small; Rebina House Care Centre's 17-review profile offers a far more complete picture of the group's care quality. If you are comparing Rebina Sunrise options, visiting both Rebina House and Sunrise Care Centre on the same trip is practical given their proximity on the same street. Ask about bed availability and fee structures across all three group entities when you speak with the operator — placement flexibility across the cluster may be available.

facilities.title: Rebina Home Care Centre Sdn Bhd
facilities.editorial_summary:
Rebina Home Care Centre Sdn Bhd is the third registered entity in the Rebina Sunrise Elderly Care group, which has operated in Johor Bahru since 2003. The group's founding mission was to address a shortage of reliable nursing care in JB, and it has since expanded to three registered facilities clustered in and around Taman Kebun Teh. The entity name suggests a home-care or outreach focus, consistent with the group also running Sunrise Homecare — though the precise split between residential beds and visiting-care services across the three entities is best clarified directly with the operator.

The Rebina Sunrise group covers both residential care and home-care services through three registered entities: Rebina House Care Centre (15 and 17 Jalan Keranji, JKM registration JM0763235-M), Sunrise Care Centre Sdn Bhd (48 Jalan Keranji, SSM 567396-U), and Rebina Home Care Centre Sdn Bhd. Operating languages are predominantly Mandarin and English. The group also runs Sunrise Homecare as a separate home-care arm — a practical option for families who may later want to shift from residential to at-home support, or who need transitional care after hospital discharge. Pricing and bed capacity are not published online; ask for a written quote and a facility tour when you visit.

Rebina Home Care Centre Sdn Bhd has 2 Google reviews averaging 3.5 stars. Given the very small review count, this figure is not a useful standalone signal; Rebina House Care Centre's 17-review, 4.8-star profile is a far better reference for the group's overall care quality. When calling, clarify whether this entity offers residential beds, home-care visits, or a combination, and ask which JKM registration covers the specific service you need before arranging a visit.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan
facilities.editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan is the flagship residence of Persatuan Kebajikan Nur Ehsan Negeri Johor (PKNENJ), a registered welfare NGO that has been active since November 2008. The home is located in Kempas Baru, Johor Bahru — an industrial-residential corridor on the western side of the city — and serves as the network's headquarters and primary point of contact for welfare referrals across all four Nur Ehsan homes in Johor.

Nur Ehsan homes are operated by Persatuan Kebajikan Nur Ehsan Negeri Johor (PKNENJ), an NGO registered with the Registrar of Societies. Admission is restricted to Muslim elderly individuals who are destitute, abandoned, or without family support — including those with physical or mental disabilities — and requires a welfare assessment by the association. These are not commercial nursing homes: fees, if any, are welfare-subsidised and depend on individual circumstances. The network now spans four homes across Johor. If your family member meets the eligibility criteria, contact the Kempas Baru headquarters to begin the referral and assessment process before approaching any individual branch.

The Kempas Baru home holds a 4.3-star rating from 19 Google reviews — a credible base for a welfare NGO. Reviewers note the Islamic communal environment and the care extended to residents who would otherwise have no family support. As a welfare facility, Nur Ehsan does not operate on a commercial basis; families should not approach it expecting the same intake process as a private nursing home. Begin with a direct call to confirm whether a family member qualifies under the association's eligibility criteria, what supporting documents are required, and whether capacity is currently available at this or another branch.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan Tangkak
facilities.editorial_summary:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Nur Ehsan Tangkak is the Ledang District branch of Persatuan Kebajikan Nur Ehsan Negeri Johor (PKNENJ), a registered NGO that has operated welfare-model elderly care homes across Johor since 2008. The branch is located in Parit Bunga, Ledang District — a rural setting in the interior of Johor, between Muar and Segamat — suited to elderly residents from the surrounding district who have no close family support.

Nur Ehsan homes are operated by Persatuan Kebajikan Nur Ehsan Negeri Johor (PKNENJ), an NGO registered with the Registrar of Societies. Admission is restricted to Muslim elderly individuals who are destitute, abandoned, or without family support — including those with physical or mental disabilities — and requires a welfare assessment by the association. These are not commercial nursing homes: fees, if any, are welfare-subsidised and depend on individual circumstances. The network now spans four homes across Johor. If your family member meets the eligibility criteria, contact the Kempas Baru headquarters to begin the referral and assessment process before approaching any individual branch.

The Tangkak branch has no Google reviews at the time of writing, which reflects both its rural setting and the welfare rather than commercial nature of the service. This is not a facility accepting open private referrals — admission requires meeting PKNENJ's eligibility criteria through a welfare assessment process. Families in the Ledang, Tangkak, or Gemencheh areas whose elderly relatives qualify should contact the Kempas Baru headquarters first, or reach the Tangkak branch directly, to begin the assessment before expecting a placement.

facilities.title: Golden Age Care Centre
facilities.editorial_summary:
Golden Age Care Centre (GACC) is a multi-branch private nursing home chain headquartered in Muar, Johor — a town roughly 90 km north-west of Johor Bahru, distinct from the JB conurbation. The Muar flagship at 28-10 Jalan Ria 2, Taman Ria is the group's founding location, established in 2004 and incorporated in 2014. Muar is a mid-sized town with a historically significant Chinese Malaysian community and a more settled pace than the JB market, which shapes both the resident profile and the visiting rhythm at this branch.

Golden Age Care Centre was established in 2004 and incorporated in 2014. The chain holds a JKM 4-star rating at its Muar flagship — one of the stronger independent certifications for a non-metropolitan Johor operator. The group's website (gacc.com.my) was not accessible at the time of writing, and published pricing or services details were not available online. All branches operate under the Akta Pusat Jagaan (Act 506). When visiting any GACC branch, ask for that branch's specific JKM licence number — ratings are certified per branch, not chain-wide — confirm staffing ratios, and request a written fee schedule.

The Muar flagship has 8 Google reviews averaging 3.9 stars. The JKM 4-star certification held by this branch is a concrete and independently verified quality marker — ask to see the certificate number and confirm it covers the current licence period when you visit. As the chain's longest-running and most established location, the Muar flagship is the most reliable reference point for GACC's overall care standards before evaluating the satellite branches at Tangkak and Batu Pahat.

facilities.title: Golden Age Care Centre Tangkak
facilities.editorial_summary:
Golden Age Care Centre Tangkak is the chain's northern Johor branch, at 5 Jalan Teratai 2 in Taman Tangkak Jaya, Muar District. Tangkak is a small town in the interior of Johor, between Muar and Segamat, serving a predominantly rural and small-town catchment. Most placements at this branch draw from local and surrounding kampung families rather than the commuter-heavy JB market, reflecting the character of the Ledang District community it serves.

Golden Age Care Centre was established in 2004 and incorporated in 2014. The chain holds a JKM 4-star rating at its Muar flagship — one of the stronger independent certifications for a non-metropolitan Johor operator. The group's website (gacc.com.my) was not accessible at the time of writing, and published pricing or services details were not available online. All branches operate under the Akta Pusat Jagaan (Act 506). When visiting any GACC branch, ask for that branch's specific JKM licence number — ratings are certified per branch, not chain-wide — confirm staffing ratios, and request a written fee schedule.

The Tangkak branch has 3 Google reviews averaging 4.0 stars. Confirm whether the JKM 4-star rating held by the Muar flagship also applies at Tangkak — certifications are branch-specific and must be individually renewed, so ask to see the Tangkak certificate directly. Also ask about room availability, fee tiers by dependency level, and how the branch handles acute medical needs given the distance from the nearest specialist hospital. Families in the Tangkak or Ledang area will find this the most practical location in the GACC network to consider first.

facilities.title: Golden Age Care Centre Batu Pahat
facilities.editorial_summary:
Golden Age Care Centre Batu Pahat is the chain's south-Johor branch, at Lot 7887 Jalan Bukit Pasir in Batu Pahat town. Batu Pahat is a larger town than Muar or Tangkak, with access to KPJ Batu Pahat Specialist Hospital and Hospital Sultanah Nora Ismail — a meaningful factor for residents who need regular specialist outpatient appointments or who may require hospital transfer for acute episodes during their stay.

Golden Age Care Centre was established in 2004 and incorporated in 2014. The chain holds a JKM 4-star rating at its Muar flagship — one of the stronger independent certifications for a non-metropolitan Johor operator. The group's website (gacc.com.my) was not accessible at the time of writing, and published pricing or services details were not available online. All branches operate under the Akta Pusat Jagaan (Act 506). When visiting any GACC branch, ask for that branch's specific JKM licence number — ratings are certified per branch, not chain-wide — confirm staffing ratios, and request a written fee schedule.

The Batu Pahat branch has 3 Google reviews averaging 1.3 stars. That figure is based on a very small count; read each review individually in Google Maps rather than relying on the aggregate rating alone, as a single negative experience can disproportionately shape a low-volume score. When visiting, verify the JKM licence status at this specific branch, ask about current staffing levels and resident capacity, and request time with the branch manager to form a fuller picture. If you have flexibility, visiting the Muar flagship first will give you the strongest baseline for evaluating the group as a whole.

facilities.title: Graceville Senior Care Centre 美好疗养中心
facilities.editorial_summary:
Graceville Senior Care Centre (美好疗养中心) is a nursing home in the Muar area of Johor, serving the town and surrounding district. The dual English and Chinese name reflects the facility's positioning within Muar's Chinese Malaysian community — a historically significant demographic in this mid-sized town roughly 90 km north-west of Johor Bahru. Muar's established community character and more relaxed pace make it a distinct environment from the larger JB and Klang Valley nursing home market.

Graceville operates without a public website, so service details, pricing, staffing ratios, and bed capacity are not available for advance review online. The Mandarin-speaking orientation will be a meaningful comfort factor for many Chinese Malaysian families from Muar and the surrounding district. A visit in person is the most reliable way to assess room standards, care staff ratios, meal quality, and activities programming before making a placement decision. Ask for the JKM licence number and confirm whether a registered nurse is present during both day shifts and overnight.

Graceville holds a 4.8-star rating from 9 Google reviews — a strong signal for a community-facing home without a web presence, where reputation is built almost entirely through word of mouth among local families. When visiting, bring a practical checklist: licence number, monthly fee structure, dependency ceilings the home can support, visiting hours, and the protocol for hospital transfers if acute care is needed. Muar's nearest hospitals include Hospital Pakar Sultanah Fatimah; confirm the facility's transfer procedure if your family member has complex or escalating care needs.
